run_summary: Portfolio is modestly in the green (+135.79 across three 1-2 day holdings). Track record shows 59% hit rate with conf-4 calls performing at 60% — my threshold for conviction sizing should be higher. New flags are mostly sector-beta names (ONTO, AEIS, VECO in semis; AMR in energy sympathy) with no idiosyncratic catalysts — all RATIONAL/OVERDONE classifications without company-specific triggers. Two genuine plays warrant action: ALGN shows counter-tape strength with elevated volume and no filed catalyst (exploratory candidate if risk-on holds), and a AVAV re-entry flag requires strict discipline (failed the bar on both counts: conf 3, no new information beyond the same geopolitical theme I exited on). Holdings are young but intact; NTLA and PTCT re-flag as RATIONAL conf 3 after positive moves — need to assess whether to hold through horizon or trim.

entries.name: Align Technology
entries.catalyst: Apparent positioning shift / consumer discretionary risk-on; no specific filing
entries.thesis: ALGN up ~5% on a day XLV is down 0.87% is a notable relative-strength signal — the name is going up against its sector tape, with elevated volume (1.76x). No 8-K and no earnings, but the combination of risk-on + IWM strength + consumer discretionary +1.45% suggests ALGN is being re-rated as a discretionary recovery name. If risk-on persists, a beaten-down consumer-medtech can keep grinding higher as shorts cover.
entries.what_kills: Reversal of risk-on tape; XLV continues to bleed and ALGN follows; an 8-K with adverse content appears
entries.reasoning: ALGN is flagged UNDERDONE conf 3 with +4.93% move on 1.76x elevated volume while XLV is down -0.87% (against-tape strength). Setup: beaten-down consumer-healthcare name showing relative strength into IWM strength + consumer discretionary +1.45%. Flag cites 'counter-tape moves with elevated volume and no visible catalyst are often early signs of positioning shift' and notes the move is defensible as a discretionary-recovery re-rating with potential short-covering tailwinds. No company-specific 8-K catalyst, no earnings. This is a positioning/tape read, not a binary event. Confidence is only 3, and horizon is 'weeks' (not days), so this does not meet conviction bar. However, it is a coherent exploratory candidate: real tape divergence (stock up, sector down), elevated volume, named thesis (discretionary risk-on + shorts covering), and a learning opportunity about positioning shifts. Size at 6% (exploratory tier). If the risk-on tape reverses or XLV resumes weakness, thesis breaks. If IWM strength persists and consumer discretionary keeps bidding, upside has room. Entry is a calibrated test position.

red_team.weakest_link: The thesis conflates sector weakness (XLV down 0.87%) with a true positioning shift, but a single day of relative outperformance on 1.76x volume is thin evidence for a multi-week rerating without a named catalyst.
red_team.critique: ALGN's +4.93% move against XLV's -0.87% decline is real, but the five-day context shows only +3.16% total move—meaning nearly 2% of the gains came in today's elevated-volume spike. A day-to-day tape read without earnings, guidance, or an 8-K catalyst is inherently noisy; consumer-discretionary risk-on may be present, but ALGN's isolation from sector strength could as easily reflect technical bounce-back or single-day positioning rebalancing that reverses just as quickly. The haiku itself acknowledges 'no company-specific catalyst' and sizes this at 6% as a learning position—which is appropriate—but the thesis that shorts are covering a 'beaten-down' name rests on unstated conviction that the prior weakness was undeserved; no valuation or fundamental case is offered.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: Semi sector rally + Trump semiconductor onshoring narrative
entries.thesis: ONTO move is fully explained by SMH +5.76% and TSM +6.94%. As a semi-process-control name, it is high-beta to the equipment sub-sector and to the Trump 'America builds chips' narrative (TerraFab, Intel/Nvidia/Apple). Magnitude is in line with sector, not in excess of it.
entries.what_kills: A specific Onto announcement (customer loss, guidance cut) appearing would shift interpretation
entries.reasoning: ONTO is flagged as RATIONAL (not OVERDONE/UNDERDONE), confidence 3, with a five-day move of +9.96% that the flag explicitly states is 'fully explained by SMH +5.76% and TSM +6.94%'. Setup is sector beta (semi-equipment high-beta to chip-rally narrative), no idiosyncratic 8-K, move is proportional to peers KLAC, CAMT, NVMI, AEIS, VECO. This is a 'no signal' case — the move is justified and in line with the sector tape. No edge here. If ONTO announces a customer loss or guidance cut it becomes a contrarian fade, but on current facts it is sector-driven and fairly priced. SKIP.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
entries.catalyst: Semi rally; possible residual effect of 6/12 8-K
entries.thesis: AEIS is power conversion for semi-cap equipment — direct beneficiary of the chip rally. The 8-K from June 12 had only 8.01 + 9.01 items (catch-all + boilerplate), which are not severity indicators on their own. Move appears to be sector beta, not idiosyncratic.
entries.what_kills: If the 8.01 turned out to be a guidance pre-announcement (we can't read the URL contents from here), the move could be idiosyncratic and have more legs
entries.reasoning: AEIS is flagged as RATIONAL conf 3 with +9.46% five-day move on 0.77x volume (below average). Flag thesis: power-conversion name riding sector beta from chip rally; June 12 8-K had only 8.01 + 9.01 (catch-all + boilerplate, not severity signals). The flag explicitly notes 'move appears to be sector beta, not idiosyncratic' and caveat 'if the 8.01 turned out to be a guidance pre-announcement... could have more legs' — but that is speculation without catalyst URL support. The 8-K SEC link is provided, confirming it exists, but flag text clearly states the items on their own are not severity indicators. Sub-1x volume on the move is a tell for passive/sector flows rather than fundamental buying. No entry signal. If a dedicated thesis for power-conversion upside emerges (new customer win, capex cycle acceleration), revisit. SKIP.

entries.name: Intellia Therapeutics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: UniQure announcing FDA filing for Huntington's gene therapy lifting gene-editing group; NTLA Reg FD 8-K on 6/15
entries.thesis: NTLA up modestly today but a notable multi-day run (extended five-day gain) in sympathy with UniQure's announcement that it will seek FDA approval for its Huntington's gene therapy. Combined with NTLA's own 6/15 8-K (Reg FD + 8.01), this looks like a coherent, justified re-rating of gene-edit/gene-therapy regulatory optionality rather than an overreaction. Risk-off tape did not prevent the move — XBI +3% confirms group bid.
entries.what_kills: NTLA 8-K turns out to be a routine conference presentation deck with no new data; UniQure FDA process stalls or pushback from agency reappears; XBI rolls over with broader risk-off resumption.
entries.reasoning: NTLA is already an open position (bought 2 days ago, +22.38% unrealized). New flag re-flags it as RATIONAL conf 3 after a +24.59% five-day run. The thesis is intact and flagged as justified by UniQure FDA-pathway read-through + NTLA's own Reg FD disclosure. No new information beyond what has already played out. The flag is confirmatory of my existing hold, not a new entry signal. Position is young (2 days) and thesis remains coherent — standard HOLD applies. Do not re-buy. SKIP.

entries.name: PTC Therapeutics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: PTCT 8-K (8.01) filed 6/16 plus broader biotech bid
entries.thesis: PTCT up sharply on roughly 2x volume with an 8-K filed yesterday (items 8.01+9.01, i.e., 'other event' with exhibits — typically a press release for a regulatory update, partnership, or pipeline news). XBI +3% provides a supportive tape. Move appears appropriate to a non-trivial disclosure rather than excessive; without reading the exhibit I can't argue it's underdone, but the combination of own filing + group strength makes the magnitude defensible.
entries.what_kills: Exhibit is routine corporate update with no new value-driving content — would suggest the move overshot and is faded once read
entries.reasoning: PTCT is already an open position (bought 1 day ago, +4.36% unrealized). New flag re-flags it as RATIONAL conf 3 on +7.06% five-day move citing the same 8-K filed 6/16 that triggered the original entry. No new information; flag is confirmatory of existing hold. Position is very young (1 day) and thesis (company-specific 8-K disclosure into supportive XBI tape) remains intact. SKIP new entry.

entries.name: Veeco Instruments Inc.
entries.catalyst: No company-specific catalyst; sympathy with SMH -4.8% and broader semi sell-off (AVGO, TSM, NVDA all down hard)
entries.thesis: VECO ran up notably over the past week alongside the semi-equipment cohort (ONTO, AEIS up similar amounts) and is now unwinding harder than the SMH index because of low float and momentum positioning. No 8-K, no earnings catalyst — this is sector beta plus crowding unwind, and an outsized one-day drop in a name that just rallied tends to overshoot fundamentals.
entries.what_kills: If a delayed news source surfaces a VECO-specific negative (customer push-out, downgrade with channel checks), the move is fundamentally driven and not a fade.
entries.reasoning: VECO is flagged OVERDONE conf 3 with a -9.44% one-day move after a +11.92% five-day rally. Flag explicitly states this is 'sector beta plus crowding unwind' with 'no company-specific catalyst' — a fade candidate IF the sector base holds. However, the flag also notes 'when three peers (VECO/ONTO/AEIS) all rallied 12-15% in 5 days and all give back sharply together on a sector-down day with empty catalyst signals, it's almost always crowded-trade unwind rather than news.' This is a short signal, not a long signal. The -9.44% move is presented as an overextension of fundamentals. Buying into a fade is not the thesis here — shorting the crowd is. Since I do not short in this portfolio, SKIP. Could revisit as a short-fade hedge if VECO stabilizes and re-approaches the recent high, but current move is correction, not opportunity.

entries.name: Alpha Metallurgical Resources
entries.catalyst: Sympathy with XLE / oil weakness on US-Iran deal framework
entries.thesis: AMR -5.24% today and -5.89% over 5 days as XLE -3.48% on the US-Iran deal — but AMR is coking coal for steel, not thermal coal or oil. The fundamental linkage to Iran/Hormuz oil supply is weak; met coal pricing tracks Chinese steel demand and Australian export tonnages, not Brent. Below-average volume (0.77x) on the selloff suggests passive ETF/energy-complex outflows rather than dedicated fundamental selling. Setup for a relative-value bounce if energy stabilizes.
entries.what_kills: Hard coking coal benchmark prices roll over; Chinese steel demand prints weak; AMR-specific operational issue surfaces
entries.reasoning: AMR is flagged OVERDONE conf 3 with -5.24% one-day and -5.89% five-day moves on 0.77x volume (below average) due to energy-complex sympathy on Iran deal. Flag thesis: met coal has weak fundamental linkage to Iran/Hormuz oil; the selloff is passive ETF outflows, not fundamental. This is a classic relative-value bounce candidate — BUT the flag explicitly says 'wait for confirmation' (oil stabilizes, XLE firms, met coal pricing stabilizes independently). Entry now would be front-running a fade on zero new information. The flag is written as 'setup for a relative-value bounce if energy stabilizes' — that's a WATCH condition, not a BUY. Horizon is 'weeks', suggesting this is a patience play. SKIP and watch for XLE stabilization + confirmed met-coal pricing data before entry.

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Iran deal-signing headline + broader defense weakness (ITA -0.95% on risk-on day)
entries.thesis: AVAV's drone-heavy revenue mix is disproportionately exposed to active conflict demand. With Trump admin signaling Iran deal signing 'in coming days' and ITA underperforming, the de-rating could extend if a deal actually prints. No 8-K signal so this is sector/macro driven, not company-specific — but the divergence vs. a risk-on tape is notable.
entries.what_kills: Iran deal falls through (Trump-admin source said 'not 100% certain'); AVAV announces a new DoD contract; broader defense bounces as buyers treat dip as opportunity
entries.reasoning: AVAV is flagged UNDERDONE conf 3 with -7.14% one-day and -8.25% five-day moves on 0.83x volume (below average) on Iran deal-signing headline. FLAG CARRIES <re_entry_warning>: I closed AVAV 16 days ago for a -5.31% realized loss. Exit reasoning was explicit: 'Entry thesis was OVERDONE (conf 3, weeks horizon) on Iran missile headline + defense sector bid. Position is -4.56% unrealized after 3 days. New flag today re-confirms OVERDONE conf 3 with identical catalyst, but now the five-day move is 30.83% on 3.28x volume... If the setup was OVERDONE three days ago at a 10-15% run, it is definitively OVERDONE now at 30%+ on no new catalyst. Thesis is broken because the move has overextended past the point where geopolitical upside can justify hold.' I exited on the basis that a 30%+ geopolitical rally in a defense name without a contract award was unsustainable, and that a 30-50% retrace was imminent. Re-entry discipline requires BOTH: (1) confidence >= 4, and (2) genuinely new information not present at exit. Current flag is confidence 3 (FAILS bar 1 — execution layer will downgrade to WATCH anyway). Current catalyst is 'Iran deal-signing headline' — IDENTICAL to the theme I exited on 16 days ago. No new information (FAILS bar 2). The flag describes AVAV as down -8.25% over 5 days, which is the predicted retrace from my exit thesis playing out. This is not a re-entry; it is confirmation that my exit analysis was correct. WATCH and pass. If AVAV announces a material DoD contract (new information), re-evaluate at that point with fresh analysis.

entries.name: JetBlue Airways Corporation
entries.catalyst: Oil collapse on Iran ceasefire narrative + risk-on broad tape
entries.thesis: JBLU is a heavily shorted, fuel-cost-sensitive, balance-sheet-stressed airline. Combination of Iran ceasefire / oil down sharply + risk-on tape + low share price (high beta to good news) explains a double-digit move without any company-specific 8-K. Magnitude is large but consistent with the operating leverage from fuel cost relief on a thin-margin carrier.
entries.what_kills: An 8-K or news showing capital raise, covenant issue, or strategic transaction would change the read entirely; oil reversing hard on Iran deal breakdown

entries.name: Pool Corporation
entries.catalyst: Unknown — high volume but no filing or news visible
entries.thesis: Volume multiple of 5.2x on a ~2.4% move with no 8-K, no earnings, and no obvious sector catalyst is genuinely unusual. Could be analyst action, institutional rebalance, or quiet news flow we can't see — cannot construct a confident bull or bear case from available evidence.
entries.what_kills: If volume turns out to be options-expiry related or index rebalance flow, the signal is noise

entries.name: Beam Therapeutics
entries.thesis: Beam has run hard over 5 days into today's continuation. XBI is only +0.95%, so this is outperforming the biotech tape. Could be conference data anticipation, M&A speculation, or a pipeline readout — cannot identify which from available evidence. Genuinely cannot articulate either side at conf 3+.

entries.name: Saia, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no earnings; possible LTL tonnage data, downgrade, or peer preannouncement
entries.thesis: Sharp multi-day decline on no visible catalyst — no 8-K, no scheduled earnings. XLI is roughly flat (-0.14%), so this is not sector tape. Could be a monthly LTL tonnage update, competitor preannouncement, or analyst downgrade not captured in the news feed. Cannot construct a directional thesis at conf 3+ without identifying the driver.
entries.what_kills: n/a — need to identify the catalyst first

entries.name: Manhattan Associates, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no earnings catalyst visible
entries.thesis: Multi-day decline in a software name with no 8-K and no scheduled earnings. XLK is only down 0.34% so this is not pure tech tape. Could be sell-side derating on supply-chain software demand concerns, a competitor read-through, or a slow-bleed sentiment shift; without an identifiable catalyst I cannot commit to overdone/underdone at conf 3+.

entries.name: Deckers Outdoor Corporation
entries.catalyst: Sector risk-off in consumer discretionary on hawkish Fed re-statement
entries.thesis: DECK down on below-average volume on a day XLY is -2.5% and the broader risk-off impulse from a more hawkish Fed is hitting discretionary hardest. No company-specific catalyst, no 8-K, no earnings. Magnitude is roughly consistent with sector beta plus mid-cap discretionary skew — nothing to fade or chase.
entries.what_kills: DECK accelerates lower on rising volume tomorrow without sector follow-through, suggesting an undisclosed name-specific issue

entries.name: Qorvo, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Sympathy with semi/Apple-supplier weakness; possible digestion of June 12 material agreement
entries.thesis: QRVO is moving roughly in line with the broader semi/Apple-supply complex on a clear sector-down day, but the June 12 1.01 filing (material definitive agreement) is non-trivial — could be M&A, a big customer contract, a debt covenant amendment, or a divestiture. Without reading the exhibit, the direction the 1.01 should push the stock is unknowable, and today's move is consistent with pure semi-tape sympathy regardless.
entries.what_kills: Without the exhibit, neither side rises above conf 3.
